STORIES.TITLE: See MIRA in action
STORIES.SUB: A quick preview of the product vibe — light, beautiful, and direct.
STORIES.HINT: Hold to pause • Click the sides to go back/forward

HOW.TITLE: How MIRA gives you control — without becoming another chore.
HOW.SUB: You talk, MIRA organizes. You decide. That’s it.

S1.D: Text, voice — your way. The key is not giving up.

S2.D: Categories, reminders, and summaries — light and easy to follow.

S3.D: A clear dashboard, beautiful charts, and tips that feel personal.

BENEFITS.TITLE: Built to bring you peace — not more anxiety.
BENEFITS.SUB: Premium design, easy reading, and microinteractions that build trust.

FRICTION.TITLE: Frictionless logging
FRICTION.DESC: Log it your way. MIRA keeps things consistent — without “punishing” you for missing a day.

CONTEXT.TITLE: Contextual insights
CONTEXT.DESC: Advice that speaks to you: less guilt, more clarity, and one simple next step.

PANEL.TITLE: Clean, premium dashboard
PANEL.DESC: Charts and cards with strong visual hierarchy. One glance and you get it.

REMINDERS.TITLE: Helpful reminders
REMINDERS.DESC: No noise. Just what matters — at the right time.

AUTO.TITLE: Automatic organization
AUTO.DESC: Categories, summaries, patterns — so you can decide with more calm.

A11Y.TITLE: Real accessibility
A11Y.DESC: Visible focus, keyboard support, and solid responsiveness.

PROOF.TITLE: You get the value in seconds.
PROOF.SUB: No cluttered screens. Just the essentials — beautiful and direct.

BULLETS.B1: Clear writing and strong visual hierarchy

TESTIMONIALS.TITLE: Real people. Real life. Real control.
TESTIMONIALS.SUB: The kind of feedback we want to hear every day.

T1.NAME: Marina
T1.QUOTE: It feels like someone finally translated money into a human language. I open it and understand.

T2.NAME: Carlos
T2.QUOTE: The dashboard is straight to the point. No clutter. It’s genuinely calming to see the month like this.

T3.NAME: Patrícia
T3.QUOTE: The microinteractions matter. It feels premium — and it makes me want to use it.

T4.NAME: Renato
T4.QUOTE: I always drop finance apps. This one… I feel like I can stick with it.

FAQ.TITLE: Frequently asked questions
FAQ.SUB: Everything clear — just how we like it.

Q1.A: If you want to organize your month without spreadsheets and stress, yes. MIRA helps you log, understand, and decide with more calm.

Q2.Q: Do I need to be “good with money”?
Q2.A: No. The idea is to keep it simple: you speak your way and MIRA gives you clarity and next steps.

Q3.Q: Can I use it on my phone?
Q3.A: Yes. The experience is mobile-first: fast, comfortable, and delightful to navigate.

CTA_FINAL.TITLE: Ready to feel “my money is under control”?
CTA_FINAL.SUB: Start for free. No card. No commitment.

ONBOARDING.TITLE: What do you want to improve?
ONBOARDING.SUB: Pick your focus and in 2 minutes MIRA analyzes your life and gives you a personalized plan.
ONBOARDING.PROMISE: In 2 minutes MIRA analyzes your life

CROSSINSIGHTS.TITLE: Cross-life insights
CROSSINSIGHTS.SUB: MIRA connects your money, schedule and routine data — and returns patterns nobody else sees.
CROSSINSIGHTS.I1: On days you sleep less, you spend 23% more
CROSSINSIGHTS.I2: When your schedule is empty, you buy more online
CROSSINSIGHTS.I3: Monday is your peak financial productivity day
CROSSINSIGHTS.NOTE: Nobody is doing this well — and MIRA does.

PRICING.FOUNDING_DESC: Lock in your lifetime price now. Only for early users. It goes up after.

PLAN.TITLE: Explore our plan
PLAN.H: Everything you need to manage your month — no spreadsheets.
PLAN.P: MIRA is built for you to log fast, understand what’s happening, and make better decisions calmly. No cluttered screens. No “pressure”. Just clarity.

PROMPTS.ADJ: Analyze my spending over the last 30 days and give me 3 practical tweaks.
PROMPTS.SAVE: Where can I save without disrupting my routine?
PROMPTS.PLAN: Create a simple 30-day plan to get my budget under control.
PROMPTS.CAP: Identify “leaks” and set a weekly cap per category.

TOAST.ACTIONS_MSG: A quick actions menu goes here (soon).
TOAST.VOICE_TRANSCRIBED: Mock transcript added to the input.

VOICE.TRANSCRIPT: Analyze my spending and tell me where I can save this week.

REPLY.QUICK: Alright. (Mock) I'll give you 3 quick, clear actions to apply today.
REPLY.DETAILED: Perfect. (Mock) I'll break it down by category and suggest tweaks with weekly targets + next steps.

INSIGHTS.TITLE: Tips & Insights
INSIGHTS.SUB: Kick-off • mock content • a clear view of what you can improve

EMPTY.TITLE: No insights yet
EMPTY.DESC: In the Kick-off this is simulated. Later, the API will bring personalized recommendations from your spending.

ERROR.TITLE: Couldn’t load
ERROR.DESC: Error simulation (Kick-off). In production it will come from the API.

GRID.HINT_NONE: No insights for this category in the selected period.

INSIGHTS.NOTE: TODO (post Kick-off): connect transactions/insights API, category limits, and history (compare with last month).

FASTFOOD_OVER.TITLE: Fast food is above target in this period
FASTFOOD_OVER.BODY: You spent {{total}} on fast food in the last {{days}} days. A small cut of {{cut}} already improves the month without feeling tight.
FASTFOOD_OVER.WHY: Fast food is often an invisible expense (small and recurring). Reducing just a part lowers the total with minimal effort.

FASTFOOD_OK.TITLE: Fast food is under control
FASTFOOD_OK.BODY: Your fast food spending is {{total}} in this period. Keeping it this way helps prevent budget leaks.
FASTFOOD_OK.WHY: When this category stays stable, you gain predictability and reduce the chances of blowing your month.

SUBS.TITLE: Subscriptions: worth a 10-minute check
SUBS.BODY: Subscriptions totaled {{total}} in this period. If there’s one service you barely use, pausing or swapping it frees up room.
SUBS.WHY: Subscriptions are quiet and add up. A quick review prevents paying for things you don’t use.

MARKET.TITLE: Groceries: one simple rule to reduce waste
MARKET.BODY: Groceries are {{total}} in this period. Before you go, list 6 essentials + 2 “free picks”. It reduces impulse buying.
MARKET.WHY: A short list keeps you focused and reduces spending driven by promotions you don’t actually need.

WEEKLY.TITLE: 8-minute check-in (once a week)
WEEKLY.BODY: Pick a fixed day: review total spend, the top category, and one simple tweak for next week.
WEEKLY.WHY: A short review prevents surprises and increases the feeling of control without turning into endless spreadsheets.

PROFILE.HINT: TODO: persist via API when endpoints arrive.

FINANCE.TITLE: Finance
FINANCE.SUB: Base for limits, alerts and recommendations (mock). Later comes from the API.
FINANCE.HINT: Tip: even approximate numbers make insights much more useful.

WEEKLY_EMAIL.NAME: Email weekly summary
WEEKLY_EMAIL.DESC: Receive an email with key points (mock).
